prose:
Property: Total electronic energy (E_total) of ethanol, units eV.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding), no Gaussian basis.
Geometry provenance: starting coordinates supplied in ethanol.xyz; fully relaxed at the same level using chemkit geometry-optimize (ASE BFGS, fmax 0.05 eV Å⁻¹), 13 steps, converged.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: 0 (neutral), 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: xtb 6.x via chemkit backend.
Convergence: converged = true, fmax criterion met; n_steps = 13. No engine warnings reported.
Live log: written during run to the default chemkit .out file in the working directory (path echoed at job launch).

The calculation therefore gives a total electronic energy of −310.05515 eV.

This value is a semi-empirical absolute energy; compare only with other GFN2-xTB energies, not with ab-initio totals whose zeros differ.
